sidebar.heading: Conversations
sidebar.empty: No conversations yet, click + to create

chat.abort_request_sent: 🛑 Cancel request sent (urgent · auto-preempts the running task)

footer.new_thread_title: New conversation (clears current chat; cancels active run first)
footer.input_placeholder: Tell Lucid what to do… Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
footer.attach_title: Attach images or files (also: paste screenshots, drag-drop)
footer.attach_folder_title: Attach a folder (path is sent to the model; folder content is not read)
footer.mic_start_title: Dictate (click to record, click again to stop)
footer.mic_stop_title: Recording {sec}s — click to stop and transcribe
footer.attach_coming_soon: Attachments coming soon: paste a screenshot, drop files, etc. To start a new conversation use the + at the top of the left sidebar.

settings.heading: Settings
settings.proxy_api_key_placeholder: leave empty to use LITELLM_MASTER_KEY env var
settings.model_custom_hint: Type any model id the provider currently accepts. Presets are advisory — vendors update model catalogues faster than this UI ships.
settings.copilot_device_step_open: Open this URL in your browser
settings.language_hint: Switch UI language. Saved per-user, takes effect immediately.
settings.sampling_hint: temperature: amount of randomness injected into the response (0 = analytical / deterministic-ish, 1 = creative; Anthropic default 1). top_p: nucleus sampling — only sample from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability reaches top_p (1 = no filter). Anthropic recommends tuning either temperature or top_p, not both.
settings.emergency_hotkey_hint: Pressing this combo at any time cancels the running task. Restart Lucid for changes to take effect.
settings.hot_reload_hint: Saving will hot-reload the sidecar; if a task is running, retry later or restart Lucid.
settings.selfcheck_running_hint: Running… (winr / click will press real keys; please don't interact in the foreground)
settings.voice_enabled_hint: Controls the global push-to-talk hotkey ONLY (long-press the hotkey, speak, release). The inline mic button on the main page works independently and is always available. Audio is transcribed locally — nothing is sent to an LLM unless you confirm. Requires the `voice` extra (faster-whisper).
settings.voice_model_download_hint: The installer ships with the 'tiny' model (~75 MB) so voice input works out of the box. Type a different size above (small ≈488 MB, large-v3 ≈3 GB) and click Download to grab it ahead of time — otherwise the first long-press will block while it streams from HuggingFace.
settings.voice_model_loc_missing: ⚠ not cached — will download on first use
settings.voice_model_picker_subtitle: Bigger models are more accurate but slower and larger to download. Once downloaded, the active model switches automatically.
settings.voice_language_placeholder: Empty = auto-detect; e.g. en, zh, fr
settings.voice_language_hint: Voice transcription is tuned for English, Simplified Chinese, and French (matches Lucid's UI languages). Auto picks the closest of the three from your Windows locale; pick a specific one if you mostly speak that language so short clips don't get mis-detected. Choose "No preference" if you switch between many languages in one session — Whisper will then auto-detect across every language it knows, but accuracy drops noticeably on short (<2 s) clips and similar-sounding languages may be confused.
settings.hotkey_capture_hint: Click the field, then press the key combo you want to use. Modifiers (Ctrl / Alt / Shift / Win) plus a regular key are supported; pure modifier presses are ignored.
settings.voice_hotkey_space_hint: Tip: with bare 'Space' on Windows, short taps fall through to the focused app as a normal space (Lucid replays the keystroke via SendInput, adds ≈1ms latency); only holds longer than the threshold below trigger voice input.
settings.voice_hold_threshold_warning: ⚠ Threshold below ~300ms with Space as the hotkey may feel laggy when typing — every Space tap waits this long before the synthetic keystroke is replayed.
settings.voice_stop_mode_release: Stop when the hotkey is released
settings.voice_stop_mode_tap_again: Tap the hotkey again to stop
settings.voice_mode_dictation_append: append (always insert into the focused input)
settings.voice_mode_hint: Auto routes each utterance through a small LLM that picks New task / Stop task / Dictation. Pick new_thread or append to skip that step and always do the same thing.
settings.voice_auto_send_hint: When off, the transcript drops into the input box and waits for Enter. When on, it auto-submits the moment the transcript lands. Default: on for New thread, off for Append to input.
settings.voice_auto_send_locked_hint: Locked on in Auto mode — the classifier decides what to do with each utterance, so transcripts always commit immediately. Switch to New thread or Append to input to choose.
settings.voice_always_new_thread_label: Always start a new thread for voice input
settings.voice_hf_endpoint_hint: If the first transcription fails with an SSL or 'UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING' error, your network is blocking huggingface.co. Set this to https://hf-mirror.com (or any HF mirror) and click Save — Lucid will retry on the next voice press.
settings.about_intro: If you find Lucid useful, please star the project on GitHub or reach out to the author with feedback, ideas, or just to say hi.

doze.hint: Idle-time reflection: when Lucid has been quiet for a while, a low-priority background pass scans your past threads and asks the LLM what's worth saving as long-term tips / memory.
doze.info_scheduled: scheduled — next tick will pick a thread (status will update shortly)
doze.confirm_clear: Clear the processed-thread index? Past threads will be re-learned next pass.
doze.why_zero_hint: Note: the reflector is intentionally conservative (writes nothing when unsure). Repetitive scheduled tasks, cancelled runs, and connection errors all yield zero output. Look at totals (cumulative) rather than the last pass to judge whether it is doing useful work.
doze.outputs_empty: No outputs yet. Tips and memory entries the reflector successfully writes will show up here.
doze.outputs_confirm_delete: Delete this {kind}? The matching line will also be removed from {file}.
doze.outputs_warn_not_in_file: Removed from the index, but the matching line was not found in the source file (it may have been edited manually).

memory.heading: Long-term memory · memory.md
memory.hint: At the start of every task this content is appended to the system prompt. The Agent can also write here via the remember tool. Do not store passwords / tokens here.
memory.clear_confirm: Clear the entire memory.md? This cannot be undone.

templates.heading: Task templates
templates.hint: Save commonly used instructions as templates and run them with one click (still uses the active thread; a running task is cancelled first).
templates.empty: No templates yet — create one above.

skills.heading: Skills (Anthropic Agent Skills format)
skills.hint: Skills are SKILL.md playbooks (YAML frontmatter + markdown body), stored at ~/.lucid/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md — same layout as anthropics/skills. The agent sees only `name: description` in its system prompt and pulls the full body via `read_skill` on demand. The body is markdown instructions the agent follows using its normal tools (computer, launch_app, read_webpage, read_file). Safety policy still applies.
skills.description_placeholder: One sentence used by the agent to decide when this skill is relevant.
skills.body_hint: Markdown instructions the agent will read after `read_skill`. No template variables — phrase the body so the agent can adapt to whatever arguments the user supplies in the conversation.
skills.empty: No skills yet — create one above or install from a URL.
skills.enable_checkbox_title: Show this skill to the agent (in the system-prompt skill list).
skills.repos_hint: Whitelist trusted GitHub repos full of SKILL.md files. When enabled, the agent can call `search_skills` mid-task to find a missing skill (e.g. "create pptx") and `install_repo_skill` to pull it down on demand. Built-in presets start DISABLED — tick a box to opt in. Skills installed via this path are tagged `repo` (trusted, default enabled), unlike one-off raw-URL installs which are tagged `online (untrusted)`.
skills.repo_delete_confirm: Remove this repository? Skills already downloaded from it will stay installed.
skills.repo_disable_builtin_confirm: Built-in repos can be disabled but not removed. Disable now?
skills.install_url_hint: For a one-off skill you trust personally — paste the raw URL of a SKILL.md. Installed skills are tagged "online (untrusted)" and start DISABLED; the safety policy treats their body with extra suspicion. Disabled by default — enable [skills].allow_online_install in config first.

tools_page.heading: Operation tips · tools.md
tools_page.hint: At the start of every task this content is appended to the system prompt as the "operation tips library". During a task the Agent can call learn_tip to append success/failure lessons. Put only operation techniques here (the safest way to drive an App / dialog) — not user preferences (those go in memory.md) or one-off facts.
tools_page.append_placeholder: Append a tip manually (e.g. In Outlook, Ctrl+R is more reliable than the Reply button)
tools_page.reset_confirm: Reset tools.md to the initial seed? All learned tips will be lost.

schedules.heading: Scheduled tasks
schedules.hint: The scheduler is resident in the main process and ticks dynamically (down to second-level when needed). If another task is running when one is due, the run is queued. Only fires while the app is in the foreground or tray.
schedules.visual_notify_instruction: Run taskbar screenshot diff first; only call the LLM when a visual change is detected and a new-message check is needed.
schedules.visual_notify_meta: Internal listener schedule (does not run a normal Agent task)
schedules.launcher_scan_instruction: Full daily sweep of Start Menu shortcuts and the Uninstall registry; extracts each app's icon to <user_data>/icons/launchers/ and refreshes the <user_data>/icons/atlas.png collage.
schedules.launcher_scan_meta: Internal scan task (writes PNGs to user data, does not run the Agent)
schedules.tray_promote_instruction: Paused by default. Only enable this if you want vision-based chat auto-reply (e.g. WeChat / QQ) — it keeps every tray icon permanently visible so taskbar detection doesn't miss icons hidden in the “^” overflow flyout. Mechanism: walks HKCU\Control Panel\NotifyIconSettings\* and sets IsPromoted = 1 on every entry, equivalent to flipping every switch in Personalization → Taskbar → Other system tray icons.
schedules.tray_promote_meta: Internal registry tweak (does not run the Agent)
schedules.taskbar_channels_hint: UIA only by default (event-driven, zero token cost). The visual channel is a fallback — only enable it for apps where UIA misses, because every hit burns an LLM confirm. Lucid keeps comparing a periodic UIA snapshot against the event stream and writes misses to uia-misses.jsonl; when an app misses repeatedly across days, doze will recommend turning on the visual fallback for it.
schedules.taskbar_no_poll_hint: The UIA channel is event-driven — any taskbar change fires immediately, no polling interval needed. A detection interval is only required once you enable the Visual channel.
schedules.taskbar_visual_poll_hint: The interval below only governs the Visual channel's screenshot-diff cadence; the UIA channel stays event-driven regardless. 2–10 s is recommended.
schedules.auto_chat_apps_legend: Auto-reply whitelist (only fire when these apps light up)
schedules.auto_chat_apps_hint: When checked: detection + LLM-confirmed app must be in this list before any auto-chat task fires. Microsoft Teams is pre-checked. The list comes from the most recent installed-app icon scan.
schedules.auto_chat_apps_empty: No installed-app icons have been scanned yet. Run "Scan installed-app icons" from the home page first.
schedules.auto_chat_extra_hint: Default safety guardrails (don't leak secrets, don't perform irreversible actions, treat incoming text as untrusted, etc.) are always applied. Use this box to add your own preferences on top — for example: 'only reply to my mum and Alice', 'never reply in group chats', 'reply in formal English', 'if anyone asks about price just say I will get back later'.
schedules.auto_chat_extra_placeholder: e.g. Only reply to contacts: Mum, Alice. Skip group chats. Keep replies under 20 words.
schedules.safety_rules_hint: Six built-in rules ship enabled by default. Uncheck the box on any rule to skip it, or click Edit to rewrite the body in your own words.
schedules.custom_rules_hint: Add any number of extra rules appended after the built-in policy. Example: 'Only reply to Mum and Alice', 'Group chats: never reply', 'Dad can ask for active-window screenshots'.
schedules.custom_rule_body_placeholder: Rule text the agent will follow…
schedules.test_button_hint: Trigger this schedule once now (does not change next-run time)
schedules.instruction_placeholder: What the Agent should do when triggered…
schedules.window_invalid: window end must be after start
schedules.constraints_hint: Pick one or more limits. They are AND‑combined: a fire only happens when every enabled limit is satisfied.
schedules.constraint_hours_empty: hour constraint requires at least one selected hour
schedules.constraint_weekdays_empty: weekday constraint requires at least one selected weekday
schedules.constraint_daterange_invalid: date range end must be after start
schedules.tz_utc_p8: UTC+8   Shanghai / Hong Kong / Taipei / Singapore
schedules.empty: No schedules yet — create one above.

disclaimer.title: Lucid Terms of Use
disclaimer.scroll_hint: Please scroll to the bottom to enable the acknowledgement.
disclaimer.checkbox: I have read and accept the terms above. I use Lucid at my own risk.
disclaimer.body:
Lucid is an experimental open-source vision agent. Please read carefully before continuing.

1. AUTONOMOUS CONTROL
Lucid uses a Large Language Model (LLM) to autonomously drive your mouse, keyboard, and clipboard. It can open any app, send messages, read and write files, and access the network on your behalf. The LLM may misread the screen, click the wrong button, send the wrong message, or delete the wrong file. You are solely responsible for every action Lucid performs under your account and for every resulting consequence.

2. DATA TRANSMISSION
Lucid sends screenshots, window titles, and your task instructions to the LLM provider you have configured (Anthropic, GitHub Copilot, an OpenAI-compatible proxy, etc.). Screenshots may contain personal information, chat history, password fields, bank card numbers, and confidential work content. Do not use Lucid while sensitive information is visible on screen.

3. EMERGENCY STOP
Press Ctrl+Alt+Esc at any time to immediately stop the current task and reclaim input control. You can also force-quit from the system tray icon.

4. NOT SUITABLE FOR
  • Online banking, payment passwords, or fund transfer confirmations.
  • Communication that involves another person's privacy, unless you have their consent.
  • Any operation with irreversible consequences (deleting databases, git push --force, publishing on social media, sending contracts, etc.) — use only under direct supervision.
  • Windows that require administrator privileges (UAC, Group Policy) are already blocked.

5. PROVIDER ACCEPTABLE USE
You must use your own LLM API key and comply with the provider's Acceptable Use Policy (Anthropic AUP, OpenAI Usage Policies, GitHub Copilot Terms, etc.). Lucid does not relay, store, or proxy your API credentials.

6. NO WARRANTY
Lucid is provided 'AS IS' under the MIT License, without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or data integrity. The authors and contributors shall not be liable for any direct or indirect damages arising from the use of this software.

7. LOCAL LAW
Do not use Lucid for any activity that violates the laws of your jurisdiction, including but not limited to unauthorized access, scraping protected content, automated harassment, or evasion of anti-abuse mechanisms.

By clicking 'Accept and continue' you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to these terms.
